Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Glycemic control appears low (low HbA1c), and serum glucose is at the lower end of normal. Findings are consistent with possible chronic kidney disease and proteinuria; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
